Honeymoon Safari

Iconic African Safari Honeymoon

Tarangire, Ngorongoro, and Serengeti

TravelersAdas ČepasKarolina Dambrauskaitė
8–18 August 2026Tanzania
Daily Detail›
Day 1August 8
Arrival in Tanzania⌂Gran Meliá Arusha›

International Flight

AirlineSWISS (operated by Edelweiss Air)
Flight NumberLX8076
Date8 August 2026
FromZurich Airport
ToKilimanjaro International Airport
Departure9:35 AM
Arrival6:40 PM

Highlights

  • A warm welcome to Tanzania
  • Settle in and relax after your journey

Logistics

  • Your Prominent Africa representative will meet you after immigration and baggage collection.
  • Private transfer to Gran Meliá Arusha.

Suggested timing

6:40 PMArrive at Kilimanjaro International Airport
7:30–8:00 PMMeet our team and depart JRO
9:00–9:30 PMArrive and check in at Gran Meliá Arusha
Day 2August 9
Arusha to Tarangire⌂Conserve Safari Tarangire›

Highlights

  • Begin your safari with your private guide
  • Explore Tarangire on your first game drive
  • Search for elephants among Tarangire’s baobabs

Logistics

  • Meet your guide after breakfast and safari briefing.
  • Travel from Arusha to Tarangire National Park.
  • Enjoy a game drive as you travel to camp; lunch timing will follow the final operating plan.

Suggested timing

MorningBreakfast and safari briefing
Late morningDrive toward Tarangire
AfternoonGame drive and transfer to camp
EveningDinner and safari orientation
Day 3August 10
Full day in Tarangire⌂Conserve Safari Tarangire›

Highlights

  • Search for Tarangire’s celebrated elephant herds
  • Explore iconic baobab landscapes
  • Follow wildlife gathering near the Tarangire River
  • Watch for predators and colorful birdlife

Logistics

  • Flexible full-day safari with your private guide.
  • A picnic or lodge lunch will be arranged according to wildlife movements.
  • Return to camp before dinner.

Suggested timing

Early morningDepart for the first game drive
MiddayPicnic or lodge lunch
AfternoonContinue game viewing
SunsetReturn to camp
Day 4August 11
Tarangire to Ngorongoro Highlands⌂Ngorongoro Farm House Lodge›

Highlights

  • Travel through changing landscapes toward the highlands
  • Enjoy views of farms and local communities along the way
  • Settle in for a relaxed afternoon at the lodge

Logistics

  • Depart Tarangire after breakfast.
  • Travel toward the Ngorongoro Highlands with possible scenic or cultural stops only if confirmed.
  • Check in and prepare for an early crater departure the next morning.

Suggested timing

MorningBreakfast and departure
MiddayTravel and lunch en route
AfternoonArrive at Ngorongoro Farm House Lodge
EveningDinner and early night
Day 5August 12
Ngorongoro Crater safari⌂Ngorongoro Farm House Lodge›

Highlights

  • Descend into the Ngorongoro Crater
  • Explore one of Africa’s richest wildlife areas
  • Watch for rhinoceros on the crater floor
  • Picnic or field lunch in the crater

Logistics

  • Early guide pickup is recommended to reach the crater efficiently.
  • Carry layers for the cool highland morning.
  • Return to the lodge after the crater safari.

Suggested timing

Early morningDepart for the crater
MorningDescend and begin game drive
MiddayPicnic or field lunch
AfternoonContinue safari and ascend
Late afternoonReturn to the lodge
Day 6August 13
Ngorongoro to Central Serengeti⌂Conserve Safari Serengeti›

Highlights

  • Enter the legendary Serengeti ecosystem
  • Enjoy a game drive as you travel
  • Discover open plains and rocky kopjes

Logistics

  • Depart the highlands after breakfast.
  • Drive into Serengeti National Park and continue game viewing en route to camp.
  • Arrival time will depend on wildlife sightings and road conditions.

Suggested timing

MorningDepart Ngorongoro Highlands
MiddayLunch en route
AfternoonGame drive in Central Serengeti
EveningArrive at camp
Day 7August 14
Central Serengeti safari⌂Conserve Safari Serengeti›

Highlights

  • Explore predator-rich plains
  • Search the kopjes and river corridors
  • Watch for lion, leopard and cheetah

Logistics

  • Full safari day with flexible routing based on current sightings.
  • Your guide will coordinate lunch and rest breaks.
  • Return to camp before dinner.

Suggested timing

Early morningFirst game drive
MiddayLunch and rest
AfternoonSecond game drive
SunsetReturn to camp
Day 8August 15
Central to Northern Serengeti⌂Mara Under Canvas Tented Camp›

Highlights

  • Travel north with game viewing along the way
  • Enter the Great Migration country of the north
  • Settle into the Northern Serengeti

Logistics

  • Depart Central Serengeti after breakfast.
  • Travel north by road with game viewing en route unless final flight routing is later confirmed.
  • Check in at Mara Under Canvas Tented Camp.

Suggested timing

MorningDepart Central Serengeti
MiddayLunch en route
AfternoonNorthern Serengeti game drive
EveningArrive at camp
Day 9August 16
Northern Serengeti and Mara River⌂Mara Under Canvas Tented Camp›

Highlights

  • Search for the moving migration herds
  • Explore the plains near the Mara River
  • Watch for predators following the herds
  • Be ready for a possible river crossing, although nature never guarantees one

Logistics

  • The day remains flexible because migration movement changes daily.
  • Your guide will choose the strongest wildlife areas and river access points.
  • Expect patient observation near potential crossing zones.

Suggested timing

Early morningDepart camp
MorningTrack migration herds
MiddayPicnic or camp lunch
AfternoonContinue wildlife viewing
EveningReturn to camp
Day 10August 17
Flexible Northern Serengeti safari⌂Mara Under Canvas Tented Camp›

Highlights

  • Enjoy a second full day following the migration
  • Explore the Mara River area and surrounding plains
  • Savor your final full safari sunset

Logistics

  • Routing will be based on current herd locations and guide reports.
  • Lunch plans remain flexible to maximize wildlife time.
  • Confirm the next day’s departure route and luggage arrangements with the guide.

Suggested timing

Early morningDepart for game drive
MiddayLunch according to wildlife location
AfternoonFinal full safari drive
EveningReturn to camp and departure briefing
Day 11August 18
Final safari and onward travel›

FAQ

Open a category, then tap any question to view the answer.

General Information
Do I need a visa to enter a new country?

Visa requirements vary by destination and passport. We recommend arranging visas before travel whenever possible. Some destinations may also allow visas on arrival at international airports or land borders. Keep your passport, visa confirmation, and entry documents accessible while traveling.

What should I do if my flight gets delayed?

Contact us as soon as possible if your arrival changes. When we know about a delay, we can adjust your airport greeting and transfer plans.

Will a guide or representative meet me at the airport?

Yes. A Prominent Africa representative will meet you after you clear customs and collect your luggage. Please keep us updated if your flight details change.

What should I do if my luggage arrives late or gets lost?

Complete the airline’s delayed or lost luggage form before leaving the airport. Share the claim information with us, and we will help coordinate delivery once your luggage arrives.

How long is it from the airport to the hotel?

Transfer time depends on the airport, your hotel location, traffic, and arrival time. Your Daily Detail section shows the planned travel flow for your journey.

Money Matters
Which currency is accepted?

Tanzanian shillings are useful for small local purchases. U.S. dollars are also widely accepted at hotels, safari lodges, camps, and many tourism-related businesses. Your guide can help you decide when local currency is best.

Should I bring cash or credit cards?

Bring both. Credit cards are useful at airports, larger hotels, and many lodges, but cash remains helpful for gratuities, small purchases, and places where card service may be unavailable.

Can I use ATMs?

ATMs are available in larger cities and some airports, but they should not be your only source of spending money. Remote safari areas generally do not have reliable ATM access.

How much cash should I bring?

This depends on your personal spending, souvenirs, gratuities, and optional purchases. Carry a practical mix of smaller U.S. dollar denominations and some local currency for minor expenses.

Safety & Health Matters
Is safari travel safe?

Safari travel is generally very well managed when you follow your guide’s instructions, remain aware of your surroundings, and respect wildlife and park rules. Steve and the lodge teams are your best source for local safety guidance throughout the journey.

Can I get out of the safari vehicle?

Only when your guide confirms it is safe. Wildlife areas are not controlled environments, and animals may be closer than they appear. Always wait for your guide’s direction before opening doors or leaving the vehicle.

Do I need vaccinations or malaria medication?

Health recommendations vary by destination, season, and personal medical history. Please consult a qualified travel-medicine professional before departure and follow current official guidance for Tanzania.

Can I drink the water?

Use bottled or properly filtered water unless your guide or lodge specifically confirms that tap water is safe. Most safari properties provide drinking water in your room, dining areas, and safari vehicle.

What happens if I get sick or injured?

Tell Steve or your lodge manager right away. We can help coordinate local medical care, adjust your plans when necessary, and assist with communication and logistics.

Should I buy travel insurance?

Yes. We strongly recommend comprehensive travel insurance that includes medical treatment, emergency evacuation, delays, cancellations, and unexpected trip disruption.

💵 Gratuities & Tipping

Gratuities are an important part of the hospitality and tourism industry throughout East Africa and are a meaningful way to recognize exceptional service. While tipping is always at your discretion, it is customary and greatly appreciated by the guides, lodge staff, and hospitality teams who help make your journey memorable.

The recommendations below reflect customary practices throughout East Africa and are intended as a helpful guide rather than an expectation.

East Africa Safari
ServiceOur Recommendation
Private Safari Guide$10–25 USD per person, per day
Lodge or Camp Staff — shared tip box$20 USD per person, per day
Hotel Staff$5 USD per person, per day
Private Airport or Hotel Transfer$5 USD per vehicle

Most safari lodges and camps provide a shared staff tip box near reception. This recognizes the entire hospitality team rather than individual transactions. Your safari guide is usually tipped directly at the conclusion of your safari.

Additional Tipping Situations
ServiceOur RecommendationGuidance
Airport Porter or Baggage Handler$2 USD per bagFor assistance with luggage at arrival or departure.
Hotel Bell Staff$2 USD per bagFor delivering luggage to or from your room.
Hotel Housekeeping$1–2 USD per room, per dayLeave daily, as housekeeping staff may change.
Restaurant Server5–10% when no service charge is includedCheck the bill before adding an additional gratuity.
Taxi DriverRound up the fare or add $2–5 USD for exceptional helpEspecially appropriate when luggage assistance is provided.
Spa or Local Activity Staff$2–5 USD for good serviceUse your discretion based on the experience.
Additional Guidance
  • Bring newer U.S. dollar bills issued in 2009 or later.
  • Carry a variety of small denominations, especially $1, $5, $10, and $20 bills.
  • Cash is generally preferred for gratuities throughout East Africa.
  • Local currency is also appropriate for restaurants, taxis, and smaller gratuities.
  • Check restaurant and hotel bills for an included service charge.
  • Lodge and camp staff generally share a communal tip box.
  • Safari guides are usually tipped directly at the conclusion of your safari.
  • A sincere “Asante” (“Thank you” in Swahili) is always appreciated.
  • If you’re ever uncertain, simply ask us—we’re always happy to help.

A Final Note

Above all, gratuities should reflect your personal experience. While these recommendations represent customary practices throughout East Africa, exceptional service may inspire additional recognition, and every gratuity is a meaningful expression of appreciation for the people who work hard to make your journey unforgettable.
